candidates.why: Primary top-line revenue figure; large positive values and clearly labeled as total revenue, unlike 'Revenues' which is a small balancing/adjustment line.
'Revenues' appears to be an adjustment/balancing line with very small (even negative) amounts; kept as a secondary fallback in case of label changes over time.
This is a cost line (COGS), not revenue, so it should not be used for REVT.
A liability representing unearned revenue, not the income statement revenue figure.
Explicitly a balancing value, not the primary revenue line; should not be used for REVT.

variables.notes: Use 'Total Revenue' as main REVT; 'Revenues' only as a fallback if needed for some years.

candidates.why: Standard label for cost of goods sold / cost of sales and clearly the direct cost line under revenue.

variables.notes: Only one clear COGS line; no conflicting alternatives.

candidates.why: Personnel expenses are typical SG&A components, but there is an explicit SG&A line present and populated, which should be preferred per hierarchy rule.
Generic operating overhead; also part of SG&A-type costs but superseded by an explicit SG&A line per rules.
Explicit SG&A line, populated in recent years; per rule (a) this should be the sole XSGA row when present and populated.
Vague additional operating bucket; possible overhead, but not needed once explicit SG&A is available.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Amortization (a D&A item) embedded in SG&A; D&A is explicitly to be excluded from SG&A components for PROF purposes.

variables.notes: Per instructions, use only the explicit SG&A line since it is present and populated; exclude D&A and broader operating expense buckets.

candidates.why: Direct, clearly labeled R&D expense line with non-missing recent values; best representation of XRD.
Abbreviated R&D line, currently without visible values; included as potential historical label variation for the same concept.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Amortization of previously capitalized R&D, which is a D&A item rather than current-period R&D expense; should not be used as XRD.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development Expenses - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental amortization detail of capitalized R&D; again D&A, not the R&D expense itself.
Capitalized R&D asset on the balance sheet, not the period expense.
Accumulated amortization of capitalized development costs; balance sheet contra-asset, not expense.
Gross capitalized development costs; asset-level, not expense-level R&D.

variables.notes: Use the explicit income statement R&D expense; 'R&D' kept as a fallback label. Exclude amortization and balance sheet development cost items.

candidates.why: Primary income statement line for interest expense with consistent negative values; best candidate for XINT.
Interest on mortgage loan; could be a component of interest expense but appears unused (all nan). Use only if main line is missing for some periods.
Additional interest expense detail; secondary priority after the main 'Interest expenses' line.
Represents minority interest share of net income, not interest expense.
Same as above; equity income allocation, not interest.
Same concept; not an interest expense line.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental detail of interest on lease liabilities; can serve as a tertiary fallback if other interest labels are missing for some years.
Generic gross interest expense label, currently without visible values; reasonable fallback after more specific populated lines.
Debt balance, not an interest expense flow.
Accrued interest liability; balance sheet item, not the period expense line used for XINT.
Long-term debt principal, not interest expense.
Equity attributable to minority shareholders, not interest expense.
Same as above; equity, not interest.
Vague balance sheet item with no visible values; not clearly an expense line and likely not what we want for XINT.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Treat XINT as a single expense line with a priority list of possible labels; use 'Interest expenses' first, then fallbacks as needed.

candidates.why: Represents equity method interests in affiliates, not total or common equity of the reporting entity.

candidates.row_label: Equity owned by the shareholders in the parent company
candidates.why: Explicit common/shareholders’ equity attributable to the parent; best match for book equity (BE) as used in PROF.
Includes both parent and non-controlling interests; acceptable proxy but less precise than equity attributable to parent shareholders.
Balancing line, not the primary reported equity figure.
Balancing line; not preferred over explicit totals.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Check-line for the accounting equation, not the equity amount itself.
Total of liabilities plus equity; not the equity figure alone.

final_choice.row_label: Equity owned by the shareholders in the parent company

variables.notes: Use equity attributable to shareholders of the parent as BE; this excludes minority interests, which are captured separately as MIB.

candidates.why: Income statement share of profit attributable to non-controlling interests; not the balance sheet equity amount but confirms presence of minorities.
Duplicate concept of non-controlling interests on the income statement; again a flow, not the stock of minority equity.
Income statement total comprehensive income share; again flow-based, not the balance sheet stock.
Balance sheet line for equity attributable to non-controlling interests; primary choice for MIB.
Alternate label for the same balance sheet minority equity; good secondary fallback if the first is missing for some years.

variables.notes: Use balance sheet minority equity; include both labels as a priority list to handle potential label changes over time.
